startups.name: Resend
startups.findingKeywords: ["Email provider"]
startups.description: A simple, elegant interface so you can start sending emails in minutes. It fits right into your code with SDKs for your favorite programming languages

startups.name: OpenAI
startups.findingKeywords: ["ChatGPT", "Sora", "GPT-5", "AGI", "Sam Altman", "ChatGPT Go", "India"]
startups.description: An AI research and deployment company famous for creating foundational models like GPT-5 and the ChatGPT chatbot. Their mission is to ensure AGI benefits all of humanity. The company is consistently in the news for its multi-billion dollar valuation and its deep partnership with Microsoft. Recently, it has adopted an 'India-first' approach, launching a promotion for 12 months of free 'ChatGPT Go' and integrating UPI payments through partnerships with Razorpay and NPCI, solidifying its focus on the Indian market.

startups.name: Figure AI
startups.findingKeywords: ["humanoid robot", "Figure 03", "Helix AI", "Brett Adcock", "BMW partnership", "BotQ"]
startups.description: A startup focused on creating autonomous humanoid robots to address labor shortages in sectors like manufacturing, logistics, and retail. Its latest model, 'Figure 03', is powered by its proprietary 'Helix' neural network, enabling it to perform complex tasks. The company is in the news for its rapid progress, backing from tech giants like Microsoft and OpenAI, and its 'BotQ' manufacturing facility designed to scale production to thousands of robots per year.

startups.name: Mistral AI
startups.findingKeywords: ["Mistral Large 2", "open-weight models", "Paris AI", "Arthur Mensch", "Mistral AI Studio"]
startups.description: A Paris-based AI startup building both open-weight and proprietary large language models. Mistral positions itself as a key European competitor to US AI labs, focusing on model efficiency and customizability. It consistently makes headlines for raising massive funding rounds at a high valuation. It is also in the news for its rapid product expansion, including the launch of 'Mistral AI Studio' for enterprise developers and new specialized models like 'Codestral' for coding.

startups.name: Urban Company
startups.findingKeywords: ["home services", "appliance repair", "salon at home", "Insta Help", "Native", "Startup of the Year 2025"]
startups.description: A large platform for home services and repairs, connecting users with verified professionals for services like cleaning, plumbing, and beauty treatments. It was recently named 'Startup of the Year' at the ET Startup Awards 2025. The company is in the news for its Q2 2025 financial results, which showed strong revenue growth but also losses driven by its heavy investment in the new 'Insta Help' vertical. Its D2C 'Native' brand of smart locks and water purifiers is also a key growth area.

startups.name: Lenskart
startups.findingKeywords: ["eyewear", "sunglasses", "contact lens", "Peyush Bansal", "omnichannel retailer", "Lenskart IPO"]
startups.description: An omnichannel D2C eyewear retailer that sells prescription glasses, sunglasses, and contact lenses through its website and a large, rapidly expanding network of physical stores. The company is known for its vertical integration, controlling design, manufacturing, and distribution. It is currently dominating financial news due to its massive Initial Public Offering (IPO), which was oversubscribed multiple times, valuing the company at over Rs 70,000 crore and signaling huge investor demand.

startups.name: Zepto
startups.findingKeywords: ["10-minute delivery", "quick commerce", "online grocery", "Aadit Palicha", "Zepto IPO", "price war"]
startups.description: A quick-commerce startup that delivers groceries and other essentials to customers in 10 minutes or less, operating a network of 'dark stores'. Following a recent $450 million funding round, Zepto is in the news for initiating a price war by removing all handling and surge fees and lowering its minimum order value for free delivery. This aggressive move has intensified competition with rivals like Blinkit and Instamart as the company prepares for a potential IPO.

startups.name: Anduril Industries
startups.findingKeywords: ["defense tech", "autonomous drones", "Lattice OS", "Palmer Luckey", "UAS", "YFQ-44A", "CCA"]
startups.description: A defense technology company that builds advanced hardware and software for military applications, specializing in autonomous systems. It is frequently in the news for its work with the U.S. government. Recently, its 'YFQ-44A' Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) prototype, a type of autonomous drone, began semi-autonomous flight testing with the U.S. Air Force, marking a major milestone in AI-driven warfare. All its systems are powered by its 'Lattice OS' AI backbone.

startups.name: Qure.ai
startups.findingKeywords: ["medical imaging", "AI radiology", "health AI", "Prashant Warier", "lung cancer screening", "Top Innovator 2025", "FDA clearance"]
startups.description: An AI-powered medical imaging company that uses deep learning to analyze X-rays, CT scans, and MRIs to automatically detect diseases and abnormalities, such as tuberculosis, lung cancer, or brain bleeds. The company was recently named 'Top Innovator' at the ET Startup Awards 2025. It is consistently in the news for its numerous regulatory approvals, having recently secured its 19th FDA clearance and new patents in India, validating its technology for clinical use globally.

startups.name: Stripe
startups.findingKeywords: ["payments", "API", "Collison brothers", "Stripe IPO", "financial infrastructure", "payment gateway"]
startups.description: A financial infrastructure company for businesses. Millions of companies, from startups to large enterprises, use Stripe's software and APIs to accept payments, send payouts, and manage their online business operations. It's persistently in the news for its status as one of the world's most valuable private startups. Recent headlines are intensely focused on its long-awaited IPO plans, with widespread speculation that a public filing is imminent in late 2025 or early 2026.

startups.name: Perplexity AI
startups.findingKeywords: ["AI search", "answer engine", "Aravind Srinivas", "Snapchat", "Bharti Airtel"]
startups.description: An AI-powered 'answer engine' that challenges traditional search engines like Google. It provides direct, cited answers to user queries by synthesizing real-time information from the web. The startup is frequently in the news for its rapid growth and high-profile partnerships, including a recent major deal to integrate its answer engine into the Snapchat app. It has also expanded its reach in India through a partnership with Bharti Airtel to offer its Pro services to subscribers.

startups.name: upGrad
startups.findingKeywords: ["online degrees", "lifelong learning", "Ronnie Screwvala", "edtech unicorn", "upskilling", "M&A"]
startups.description: An online higher education platform providing industry-relevant programs, diplomas, and degrees in partnership with top global universities. It focuses on upskilling working professionals in high-growth fields like data science, technology, and management. upGrad is frequently in the news for its aggressive M&A strategy, having acquired several other edtech companies to consolidate its market position. It also makes headlines for its international expansion, particularly in APAC and Europe, and for raising significant funding.

startups.name: Postman
startups.findingKeywords: ["API platform", "developer tools", "SaaS unicorn", "Abhinav Asthana", "API lifecycle", "collaboration"]
startups.description: An API platform for building and using APIs. Postman simplifies each step of the API lifecycle and streamlines collaboration, enabling over 30 million developers and thousands of companies to create better APIs faster. As one of India's highest-valued SaaS unicorns, it's often in the news for its massive and growing developer community. Recent news highlights its continuous platform expansion, adding features for API governance, security, and management to serve large enterprise clients.

startups.name: DeHaat
startups.findingKeywords: ["agritech", "farmer network", "full-stack", "supply chain", "Shashank Kumar", "phygital"]
startups.description: A full-stack agritech platform offering end-to-end solutions and services to India's farmers. It operates a 'phygital' (physical + digital) model, providing agricultural inputs like seeds and fertilizers, customized farm advisory, financial services, and market linkages for selling produce directly to large buyers. DeHaat is regularly in the news for its rapid expansion across India, its successful funding rounds, and its impact on building an efficient farm-to-fork supply chain by aggregating millions of farmers.

startups.name: Shiprocket
startups.findingKeywords: ["logistics aggregation", "e-commerce shipping", "D2C enablement", "Saahil Goel", "Shiprocket IPO", "fulfillment"]
startups.description: A tech-enabled logistics and fulfillment platform designed for India's D2C brands and SMEs. It acts as an aggregator, integrating with multiple courier partners (like Delhivery, Blue Dart) on a single platform. This allows sellers to manage shipping, track orders, and handle returns seamlessly. As a logistics unicorn, it's in the news for its IPO preparations and its strategic acquisitions (like Pickrr) to consolidate the e-commerce enablement market. It is also expanding into cross-border shipping and B2B fulfillment.

startups.name: Ola Electric
startups.findingKeywords: ["electric scooter", "S1 Pro", "Futurefactory", "Bhavish Aggarwal", "Ola IPO", "Gigafactory", "EV battery"]
startups.description: An electric vehicle manufacturer, known for its S1 line of electric scooters, which has captured a significant portion of India's EV scooter market. The company is building a vertically integrated EV ecosystem, including its own 'Futurefactory' for scooter production and an ambitious 'Gigafactory' for battery cell manufacturing. It is constantly in the news for its aggressive market-share gains, new product launches (including electric bikes and cars), and its high-profile, much-anticipated IPO.

startups.name: Dream11
startups.findingKeywords: ["fantasy sports", "Dream Sports", "IPL", "online gaming", "Harsh Jain", "gaming regulations"]
startups.description: A fantasy sports platform that allows users to create virtual teams of real-life players and earn points based on their actual performance in sports like cricket, football, and kabaddi. As the flagship brand of the Dream Sports portfolio, it is the leader in India's massive fantasy gaming market. It is frequently in the news during major sporting events like the IPL and Cricket World Cup, and is also at the center of ongoing legal and regulatory discussions regarding online gaming taxation and classification as a 'game of skill'.

startups.name: Agnikul Cosmos
startups.findingKeywords: ["spacetech", "satellite launch", "Agnibaan", "3D printed engine", "private spaceflight", "IN-SPACe", "Agnilet"]
startups.description: A space technology startup that designs and manufactures small-satellite launch vehicles. Their flagship rocket, 'Agnibaan', is a highly customizable rocket designed to carry payloads up to 300 kg. It is notable for its 'Agnilet' engine, which is the world's first single-piece, 3D-printed rocket engine. As a pioneer in India's private space sector, it is in the news for its successful sub-orbital test launch, which validated its 3D-printed engine technology and moves it closer to commercial orbital launches.

startups.name: Wiz
startups.findingKeywords: ["cloud security", "CNAPP", "vulnerability management", "agentless", "Assaf Rappaport", "SaaS", "cybersecurity"]
startups.description: A cloud cybersecurity company that provides a platform for enterprises to secure their entire cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP). Its agentless platform connects in minutes and scans the full cloud stack to identify vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and active threats. It is renowned for being one of the fastest-growing SaaS companies in history. It's constantly in the news for its astronomical valuation (reportedly over $12 billion) and its intense competition and M&A battles with security giants like Palo Alto Networks.

startups.name: Zomato
startups.description: Zomato is an Indian online food discovery and delivery startup founded in 2008. It enables users to explore restaurants, read reviews, and order food through its platform. Over time, Zomato expanded into online delivery, dining services, and quick-commerce, becoming a major player in India’s foodtech industry.

startups.name: Swiggy
startups.findingKeywords: ["food delivery", "Swiggy Instamart", "IPO", "Dineout", "Sriharsha Majety", "quick commerce", "logistics"]
startups.description: A leading on-demand convenience platform in India. While it started as a food delivery service, it has aggressively expanded into quick-commerce with 'Swiggy Instamart', restaurant discovery and booking with 'Dineout', and hyper-local package delivery with 'Swiggy Genie'. The company is dominating headlines as it prepares for a large-scale, much-anticipated IPO. News also focuses on its intense competition with Zomato, its profitability milestones in the core food business, and the rapid growth of its Instamart arm.

startups.name: Groww
startups.findingKeywords: ["investing app", "stocks", "mutual funds", "IPO", "Demat Account", "NextBillion"]
startups.description: A digital-first financial services platform that allows users to invest in stocks, mutual funds, F&O, and other financial products. It has grown rapidly by simplifying the investment process for millions of new, young investors. The company, under its parent Billionbrains Garage Ventures, is currently in the news for its ongoing Initial Public Offering (IPO). The IPO has seen strong subscription numbers, and analysts are closely watching its valuation and performance as a key indicator of the public market's appetite for fintech.

startups.name: Paytm
startups.findingKeywords: ["Paytm stock", "Vijay Shekhar Sharma", "RBI regulatory ban", "Paytm crisis", "One97 Communications", "Paytm UPI market share"]
startups.description: The most volatile stock in Indian Fintech. Generates daily news due to regulatory battles with the RBI, stock price fluctuations, and leadership changes. Essential for analyzing 'Turnaround' vs 'Crisis' sentiment.

startups.name: Byju's
startups.findingKeywords: ["Byju Raveendran", "Byju insolvency", "NCLT court case", "Byju valuation cut", "edtech layoffs", "BCCI dispute"]
startups.description: The biggest corporate governance story in India. Perfect for negative sentiment analysis. News flows daily regarding court cases, insolvency proceedings, and investor disputes.

startups.name: Meesho
startups.findingKeywords: ["Meesho IPO", "Vidit Aatrey", "Amazon vs Meesho", "zero commission model", "softbank investment", "profitability"]
startups.description: Currently preparing for a massive $600M+ IPO. News volume is high as financial analysts compare its 'profitability' claims against Amazon and Flipkart. A strong 'Pre-IPO' sentiment candidate.

startups.name: Mamaearth (Honasa)
startups.findingKeywords: ["Honasa Consumer", "Ghazal Alagh", "Mamaearth share price crash", "unsold inventory", "distributor controversy", "Varun Alagh"]
startups.description: A prime example of post-IPO volatility. Recent stock crashes due to 'inventory distribution' issues have made it a hot topic for financial news, generating high negative/cautious sentiment.

startups.name: Ather Energy
startups.findingKeywords: ["Ather IPO", "Tarun Mehta", "Ather Rizta", "Ola Electric competitor", "electric scooter market share", "Hero MotoCorp investment"]
startups.description: The main rival to Ola Electric. With their IPO filed/upcoming, the media constantly compares Ather's 'quality' vs Ola's 'quantity'. Great for 'Competitor Analysis'.

startups.name: boAt Lifestyle
startups.findingKeywords: ["Aman Gupta", "boAt IPO", "Sameer Mehta", "wearables market share", "Made in India", "smartwatch data breach"]
